report_name: 'Ghostcommit' Attack Hides Prompts in Images to Trick AI Agents into Leaking Secrets

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: Malicious prompts are hidden as text within a PNG image file, which are then read and executed by a multimodal AI coding agent. This is a form of indirect prompt injection.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attack payload, consisting of a markdown file and a PNG with a hidden prompt, is committed to a source code repository to become a persistent threat waiting for an AI agent to activate it.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Secrets read from .env files are exfiltrated by being encoded as integer tuples and committed back into a source code file in the repository, using the repository as the web service for exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: Exfiltrated secrets are encoded as a tuple of integers within a source code file to evade detection by traditional secret scanners that look for string patterns.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: The attack targets sensitive data, such as API keys, stored in `.env` files within the code repository.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.description: An AI coding agent that was found to be vulnerable to the Ghostcommit attack, executing malicious prompts hidden in images.

tools.name: Antigravity
tools.description: An AI coding agent mentioned as being vulnerable to the Ghostcommit attack.

tools.name: CodeRabbit
tools.description: A text-based AI code review tool that was bypassed by the Ghostcommit attack because it could not analyze the content of the PNG image file.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: An AI coding assistant that was not vulnerable to the Ghostcommit attack, as it consistently refused the malicious instructions during testing.

tools.name: Gemma 4
tools.description: A Google AI model used by the researchers to build a prototype multimodal review tool capable of detecting the Ghostcommit attack.

campaigns.name: Ghostcommit
campaigns.description: A novel proof-of-concept supply chain attack that embeds malicious prompt-injection instructions inside PNG image files to bypass AI code reviewers and trick AI coding agents into exfiltrating secrets.
campaigns.objective: Exfiltrate secrets from development environments by tricking AI coding agents.

identities.name: University of Missouri-Kansas City's ASSET Research Group
identities.description: The research group that discovered and demonstrated the Ghostcommit proof-of-concept attack.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Code Repository Commit
infrastructure.description: A source code repository is used to host the trigger files (markdown and PNG image) for the Ghostcommit attack, which are later activated by an AI agent.

indicators.name: Ghostcommit AI Agent Access to .env File
indicators.pattern:
title: Ghostcommit AI Agent Access to .env File
id: 9b1b3c4d-5e6f-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects an AI coding agent process accessing a .env file, which may indicate a Ghostcommit-style attack.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/ghostcommit-hides-prompt-injection-in-images-to-fool-ai-agents-steal-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1552.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cursor.exe'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\.env'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects AI coding agents, such as Cursor, accessing sensitive .env files, a key behavior in the Ghostcommit attack.

indicators.name: Ghostcommit AI Agent Instruction File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Ghostcommit AI Agent Instruction File Creation
id: 8a0a2b3c-4d5e-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named AGENTS.md, which is used as a trigger in the Ghostcommit attack to direct AI agents to a malicious image.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/ghostcommit-hides-prompt-injection-in-images-to-fool-ai-agents-steal-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\AGENTS.md'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of 'AGENTS.md', a file used in the Ghostcommit attack to provide initial instructions to the targeted AI agent.

indicators.name: Ghostcommit Suspicious Commit Message
indicators.pattern:
title: Ghostcommit Suspicious Commit Message
id: 79f91a2b-3c4d-4e6f-7a8b-9c0d1e2f3a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects git commit commands with messages indicating the addition of AI agent instructions, which could be part of a Ghostcommit attack.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/ghostcommit-hides-prompt-injection-in-images-to-fool-ai-agents-steal-secrets/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\git.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'commit'
      - 'Add AI agent instructions'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects git commits with messages like "Add AI agent instructions", which warrants careful review of all files in the commit for Ghostcommit triggers.
